title: Checkpoint serial runs to avoid repeated full replay
problem: The serial runner preserves deterministic replay semantics by repeatedly merging and validating accepted deltas. At 16 papers this was tolerable, but the final post-extraction validate plus merge window was about 33 seconds and will grow for dozens or hundreds of papers.
proposed_change: Keep clean final replay for reproducibility, but add an incremental graph/checkpoint path for in-run package building, validation, and monitoring so each paper does not require full replay work.
acceptance_criteria: Serial runs can build the next package from a checkpointed graph; final clean replay still produces the same graph; audit reports checkpoint/replay consistency; per-paper validation/merge overhead remains bounded on larger pilots.

observations.context: In ag95-diverse-003-16p, events show mr4792069 extraction finished at elapsed 2859.184s, validation finished at 2876.185s, merge finished at 2892.260s, and run finished at 2892.323s. The final validate/merge window was about 33 seconds on only 16 papers.

ag95-diverse-004-replay-16p replayed 16 existing deltas without Codex and still took 295.635 seconds. Lightweight monitor samples showed per-paper replay cost growing with graph size, confirming checkpointed serial state remains important before dozens or hundreds of papers.
